2605.00123.title: Minimal, Local, Causal Explanations for Jailbreak Success in Large Language Models
2605.00123.paper_summary: Safety trained large language models (LLMs) can often be induced to answer harmful requests through jailbreak prompts. Because we lack a robust understanding of why LLMs are susceptible to jailbreaks, future frontier models operating more autonomously in higher-stakes settings may similarly be vulnerable to such attacks. Prior work has studied jailbreak success by examining the model's intermediate representations, identifying directions in this space that causally encode concepts like harmfulness and refusal. Then, they globally explain all jailbreak attacks as attempting to reduce or strengthen these concepts (e.g., reduce harmfulness). However, different jailbreak strategies may succeed by strengthening or suppressing different intermediate concepts, and the same jailbreak strategy may not work for different harmful request categories (e.g., violence vs. cyberattack); thus, we seek to give a local explanation -- i.e., why did this specific jailbreak succeed? To address this gap, we introduce LOCA, a method that gives Local, CAusal explanations of jailbreak success by identifying a minimal set of interpretable, intermediate representation changes that causally induce model refusal on an otherwise successful jailbreak request. We evaluate LOCA on harmful original-jailbreak pairs from a large jailbreak benchmark across Gemma and Llama chat models, comparing against prior methods adapted to this setting. LOCA can successfully induce refusal by making, on average, six interpretable changes; prior work routinely fails to achieve refusal even after 20 changes. LOCA is a step toward mechanistic, local explanations of jailbreak success in LLMs. Code to be released.
2605.00123.paper_summary_zh: 安全訓練的大型語言模型 (LLMs) 常常可以透過越獄提示來回答有害的請求。因為我們對於 LLMs 為何容易受到越獄攻擊缺乏穩健的理解，未來在更高風險環境中更自主運作的前沿模型可能同樣會受到此類攻擊的脆弱性。先前的研究通過檢查模型的中間表示來研究越獄成功，識別出在這個空間中因果編碼有害性和拒絕等概念的方向。然後，他們將所有越獄攻擊全局解釋為試圖減少或增強這些概念（例如，減少有害性）。然而，不同的越獄策略可能通過增強或抑制不同的中間概念來成功，而相同的越獄策略可能對不同的有害請求類別（例如，暴力與網路攻擊）無效；因此，我們尋求給出一個局部解釋——即，為什麼這個特定的越獄成功了？為了解決這一空白，我們引入 LOCA，一種通過識別一組最小的可解釋的中間表示變化來給出越獄成功的局部因果解釋的方法，這些變化因果地引導模型在一個本來成功的越獄請求上拒絕。我們在 Gemma 和 Llama 聊天模型的廣泛越獄基準上評估 LOCA，並與適應於此環境的先前方法進行比較。LOCA 通過平均進行六次可解釋的變化來成功引導拒絕；而先前的研究即使在 20 次變化後也常常無法達成拒絕。LOCA 是朝向 LLMs 中越獄成功的機械性局部解釋邁出的一步。代碼將會發布。
2605.00123.author: Shubham Kumar et.al.
